title: Global NPP Heatmap
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Compare net primary productivity across biomes using an interactive heatmap with per-square-meter and total contribution views.

learningObjectives: Compare net primary productivity across different biomes and explain why some regions are more productive than others
Identify the limiting factors that constrain productivity in each biome
Distinguish between per-area productivity and total global contribution
Explain why the open ocean is the largest total contributor to global NPP
prerequisites: Understanding of photosynthesis as the basis of primary productivity
Basic knowledge of Earth's major biomes
Familiarity with the concept of limiting factors

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Data analysis and pattern recognition through interactive visualization
keyQuestions: Why do tropical rainforests have the highest per-area NPP?
Why does the open ocean contribute more total NPP than any single terrestrial biome?
What limiting factors explain the low productivity of deserts and tundra?
How might climate change shift global NPP patterns?
commonMisconceptions: Students often assume the most productive biome per area is also the largest total contributor to global NPP
Many students underestimate ocean productivity because they focus on per-area values rather than total area
Students may believe all aquatic ecosystems have similar productivity levels
assessmentOpportunities: Compare student predictions from the warm-up with actual NPP values from the simulation
Evaluate written explanations of the per-area vs. total contribution distinction
Assess student ability to link limiting factors to productivity patterns
